works.author: hegel
works.title: The Logic of Hegel
works.licenseNote: Translation first published by Oxford University Press, 1892 (2nd ed., revised and augmented) — public domain in the USA (pre-1929). Gutenberg edition released 2017, marked public domain in the USA.
works.substitution: This is NOT the Sibree translation of Hegel's 'Philosophy of History' that the original spec named — it is William Wallace's translation of Part I ('Logic') of Hegel's Encyclopaedia of the Philosophical Sciences, also known as the 'Encyclopaedia Logic' or 'Shorter Logic' (2nd ed., 1892, Oxford). Verified directly against the plaintext: title page reads 'THE LOGIC OF HEGEL / TRANSLATED FROM THE ENCYCLOPAEDIA OF THE PHILOSOPHICAL SCIENCES / WILLIAM WALLACE, M.A, LL.D. ... SECOND EDITION, REVISED AND AUGMENTED / OXFORD UNIVERSITY PRESS / 1892'. Standard modern Gutenberg header/footer present: '*** START OF THE PROJECT GUTENBERG EBOOK THE LOGIC OF HEGEL ***' and matching '*** END OF ... ***' footer (not the older 'End of Project Gutenberg's ...' form). Plaintext URL fetched successfully (HTTP 200, ~938KB). Recommend substituting this for the Sibree work if a Hegel logic/encyclopaedia text is desired; flag to product/editorial that this changes the work's scope from philosophy-of-history to logic/metaphysics.

works.author: hegel
works.title: Hegel's Philosophy of Mind
works.licenseNote: Translation first published by Clarendon Press (Oxford), 1894 — public domain in the USA (pre-1929). Gutenberg edition released 2012, marked public domain in the USA.
works.substitution: This is NOT the Sibree 'Philosophy of History' originally specified — it is William Wallace's translation of Part III of Hegel's Encyclopaedia of the Philosophical Sciences ('Philosophy of Mind'/'Philosophy of Spirit'), with five introductory essays by Wallace. Verified directly against plaintext: title page reads 'Hegel's Philosophy of Mind / By Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel / Translated From The Encyclopaedia of the Philosophical Sciences / With Five Introductory Essays / By William Wallace, M.A., LL.D. ... Oxford / Clarendon Press / 1894'. Standard modern Gutenberg header/footer present: '*** START OF THE PROJECT GUTENBERG EBOOK HEGEL'S PHILOSOPHY OF MIND ***' and matching '*** END OF ... ***' footer. Plaintext URL fetched successfully (HTTP 200, ~744KB). Companion volume to #55108 (same translator/series); together the two Wallace volumes cover Encyclopaedia Parts I and III but do not include Sibree's separate 'Lectures on the Philosophy of History' text — that would need to be sourced separately if still wanted.

exclusions.author: rousseau
exclusions.title: The Confessions of Jean Jacques Rousseau
exclusions.reason: UNCLEAR. Text is the 1903 Aldus Society (London) private printing; Project Gutenberg's own bibrec page lists no translator, and the plaintext front matter names no translator anywhere. Gutenberg's PD-in-USA copyright status field is satisfied (pre-1929 publication), but the underlying translation's authorship/provenance cannot be confirmed from primary sources.
